name: The Heart of Change
description: "Help Tamara Day and team bring this heart to where it matters most. Your gift will help place it with families at Ronald McDonald House Kansas City."Inspired by the spirit of Laid-Back Luxe: the belief that beautiful spaces should also feel warm, welcoming, and lived in, this heart celebrates the many hands that help create comfort for families at the Ronald McDonald House. The surface is covered

name: A Warm Welcome
description: A warming welcome to Kansas City, displaying multiple things that make Kansas City special.

name: Welcome to Teal Town - KC Baby
description: The design is a vibrant, two-sided sculptural heart honoring the Kansas City Current and their trailblazing spirit. One side features dynamic portraits of players in action, layered over bold teal, red, and navy graphics with the text "Welcome to Teal Town." The reverse transforms the heart into a stylized teal-and-red soccer ball, symbolizing unity and passion for the game. Designed as both art a

name: Two States, One City
description: This sculpture represents the connection between Kansas and Missouri as the foundation of Kansas City, using sacred geometry, bold color, and symbolism to express unity and shared identity. The circle and heart shape guide the flow of the piece, symbolizing wholeness and connection. The color palette blends personal symbolism with colors inspired by local sports teams-blue (wisdom/state line), gre

name: Power Of Heart
description: Power of Heart: It is first and foremost a monument in memory of those who fought so hard, however are with us in love, spirit, and memory. Secondly, to those who are in their battle of survival; and to the supporting hearts that love, empower, cure and sustain. Additionally to those who survived, and continue to fight. Lastly, to those who dream, aspire, inspire, and empower through the beating r

name: Reminder of Reality of Life
description: I wanted to create a story on my piece about family, growth, life and death. I want people to look at this painting and understand what the story tells us. As they look at my heart design I hope they're holding onto their loved ones or friends to bring them closer.This painting to me expresses a lot and it makes me happy to be able to show the world my heart of Kansas City. It's a reminder of the

name: The Midtown Artifact
description: This design draws from the terra cotta tilework of a historical Art Deco landmark in Midtown Kansas City, built in 1929 by local architects McKecknie and Trask. By using 3D scanning and sculpting, the piece explores how technology can preserve and reinterpret fading details of the past, creating new forms from old surfaces. Embedded around a weathered circular heart, the tiles reflect the quiet en

name: The World's Game in Our City
description: The design celebrates the history of the World's Game in Kansas City. Between inaugural seasons, championship winning teams, and "first-in-the-world-to-do-it", soccer has been commanding headlines in this city for decades. This heart shows that off with jerseys that tell the stories forever ingrained in the teams', fans', and city's hearts.

name: KC Looks Good On You
location_name: W 119th St & Roe Ave, Leawood, KS 66211, USA
description: "Kansas City Looks Good on You" combines vintage "Greetings from Kansas City" postcard imagery with a heart shaped mirror at its center. The bold, curved lettering invites viewers to see themselves in the art, literally placing them in the heart of KC. This interactive design blends nostalgia and modern connection, celebrating that Kansas City truly looks good on everyone.

name: There's No Place Like Chrome
location_name: 1515 Wyandotte St, Kansas City, MO 64108, USA

name: KCurtain KCall
location_name: 518 N Main St, Grain Valley, MO 64029, USA
description: This piece is showcasing all of the brilliant performance platforms in Kansas City, on both indoor and outdoor stages. The excitement of lively productions, bright lights and amazing scenes leaves its magical mark and inspiration for audiences of all ages. From first curtain pull back to the grand finale at closing, the pageantry of the stage captures your heart and creates a world of fantasy, as

name: Somewhere Over Kansas City
location_name: 10904 E Winner Rd, Independence, MO 64052, USA
description: This heart celebrates Kansas City's vibrant spirit by blending KC's iconic architecture, El Salvadoran folk art, and the excitement of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. A city uniting together through soccer shines on the front, while the back transforms into an Oz-inspired dreamscape filled with poppies, ruby slippers, and a Kansas City inspired Emerald City. Together, the front and back showcase the hear

name: The Heart of America
location_name: 500 W US Hwy 24, Independence, MO 64050, USA
description: A rendition of patriotism with a powerful yet elegant use of brilliant candy colors and a dazzling array of gold leaf border elements with an intricate touch of airbrush work.

name: Magpie Collectors
location_name: 1609 US-50, Bates City, MO 64011, USA
description: This design features a mosaic-covered heart adorned with a 2D metal magpie perched on top and a "trinket trade" nest at its center. All decorative materials will be sourced through Magpie Creative Reuse, a local organization that promotes creative reuse and community engagement. The project invites Kansas Citians to reflect on the joy of found objects and the shared stories they carry.

name: If I Only Had A Heart
location_name: 2700 Guinotte Ave, Kansas City, MO 64120, USA
description: The moment I saw the Parade of Hearts sculpture with its hollow center, I knew it was the perfect chance to bring the Tin Man from The Wizard of Oz to life. Complete with his funnel-shaped hat, the sculpture takes on his form with riveted metal, a clock-heart, Kansas sunflowers, and the Yellow Brick Road, each symbolizing love, growth, and the journeys we take to find what we value most. I hope it

name: Kansas City Has Heart
location_name: 2715 Rochester Ave, Kansas City, MO 64120, USA
description: My heart celebrates Kansas City's diversity and warmth with the word "Welcome" displayed in 88 languages representing our community - including ASL and Braille. A bold heart at the center highlights the word "ART," surrounded by the KC skyline and the message "Kansas City Has Heart." The design invites everyone-residents, visitors, and newcomers alike-to feel at home and part of our shared story.

name: Flowers of the Heartland
location_name: 10521 Mission Rd, Overland Park, KS 66206, USA
description: Flowers of the Heartland features vibrant, unique flowers that celebrate the beauty of nature and the artistry of my work. Each flower/greenery painted reflects the heart of our community-colorful, diverse, and full of life-preserved to honor our shared stories and connections.

name: The Love of Sports Unites Us-Love All, Fear None
location_name: 1599 Iron St, North Kansas City, MO 64116, USA
description: The soccer ball colors represents the colors most frequently seen in the international flags. Around the soccer ball on each side has the primary color of our two local soccer teams and our professional American football team. In the middle of the open heart is the American sign language sign for I love you.

name: The Night the Rabbits Danced With the Moon
location_name: 7200 NW Eastside Dr, Weatherby Lake, MO 64152, USA
description: This piece celebrates the quiet, joyful connection between nature and the human experience. Echinacea reaches skyward like a child seeking comfort, while rabbits dance beneath the moon's phases, full of curiosity and play. Black and white with touches of orange, the work invites viewers to feel wonder and remember that all life-plants, animals, humans, and celestial cycles-is interconnected, growi

name: LOVE KC
location_name: 8140 N Brighton Ave, Kansas City, MO 64119, USA
description: LOVE KC with icons, stadiums and support hands on each side.

name: In the Key of KC
location_name: 15335 Old Town Dr, Parkville, MO 64152, USA
description: "In the Key of KC" honors 100 years of Kansas City jazz through bold, graphic portraits of its most legendary musicians. Set against the KC skyline and flowing sheet music, the design captures the rhythm, soul, and improvisation that define the city's sound. This heart invites viewers to celebrate the past, feel the music, and keep Kansas City's jazz legacy alive.

name: Choo Choo Chug Chug!
location_name: 100 S Main St, Parkville, MO 64152, USA
description: The "open heart" design displays the front of the Canada Pacific Kansas City and Union Pacific railroad's engine and its importance to Kansas City, Mo helping set up KC as a major railroad hub connecting KC trans continentally from Canada to Mexico and North/South, East/West in the US and to Mexico, linking it to a broader railroad network. The side of the base design displays the full side view o

name: Lovebirds
location_name: 7 W 1st St, Parkville, MO 64152, USA
description: A pair of cardinals rests within a heart-shaped window, framed by blue skies and clouds. The nest base, complete with speckled eggs, symbolizes home, love, and new beginnings.

name: Harmony Starts With The Smallest Act
location_name: 4151 N Mulberry Dr, Kansas City, MO 64116, USA
description: The hummingbird - fragile in form, yet filled with strength and purpose. It hovers between stillness and motion, symbolizing the delicate resilience that lives in us all. The hummingbird represents our journey through adversity. Each flutter of its wings echoes the heartbeat of unity, reminding us that even the smallest presence can bring light, healing, and harmony to the world. It teaches us tha

name: Rooted in Love
artist: Marie Tabler, William C. Meyer, Audrey Tabler
location_name: 201 E Gregory Blvd, Kansas City, MO 64114, USA
description: This piece reflects the grassroots creativity and organized movements that have revitalized the Kansas City community, bringing new energy and vibrance. The city skyline symbolizes prosperity, while surrounding fields and sprouting seeds represent renewal, nourishment, and resilience. At the center, a reflective heart mosaic unites these contrasts, celebrating hope, individuality, and the spirit o

name: Before the Prairies
location_name: 433 E Red Bridge Rd, Kansas City, MO 64131, USA
description: Before Kansas had its prairies, it was part of the Western Interior Seaway and was home to many ancient marine animals and pterosaurs. Kansas has been the source of important marine fossils and paleontologists. With these discoveries, we can see what things looked like before the prairies.

name: Chromatic Unity
location_name: 411 E 135th St, Kansas City, MO 64145, USA
description: I wanted to encapsulate the vast beauty and diversity of Kansas City through a new, colorful, unique, diverse and thought provoking piece. I love the idea of putting solid color lines on the edges-splashes of color on the base-rising colorful bubbles on the back & recognizable staples on the front.

name: Home Sweet Home, A Cross-Stitched Welcome to Kansas City
location_name: 12301 Blue Ridge Blvd, Grandview, MO 64030, USA
description: Paying homage to the midwestern artist's cross stitch, generally welcoming someone into their home, this piece brings a modern color palette and design elements into the traditional feminine art of stitching, by welcoming passers by "Home" to Kansas City.

name: One World, One City
location_name: 14501 White Ave, Grandview, MO 64030, USA
description: Located in the center of the US, Kansas City truly is the heartbeat of the heartland. We are a city that welcomes visitors from all over the world, and a place where everyone feels at home. Visitors experience our wholesome hearts, open arms and welcoming smiles on both sides of the state line.

name: Around Town
location_name: 724 Main St, Grandview, MO 64030, USA
description: Around Town focuses on our popular landmarks and amazing neighborhoods. The center opening of new design allows for folks to have pictures taken with themselves right in the middle of images celebrating Kansas City.

name: HEART OF THE GAME
location_name: 900 W Foxwood Dr, Raymore, MO 64083, USA
description: A heart-shaped design formed by baseball stitching within a circular frame, topped with KC lettering. The base is decorated with a pile of baseballs.

name: Blooming Afternoon
location_name: 214 S Washington St, Raymore, MO 64083, USA
description: This design is heavily inspired by Loose Park, with the focus being centered on the fountains in the pond and the rose garden.

name: Los Estados Unidos
location_name: 415 E 12th St, Kansas City, MO 64106, USA
description: Having had some states absorbed by the United States into what is now the American Southwest, Mexico has been a part of America's ascent. This includes not only the land, but the what is grown in Mexican soil, and the people who seek to work in the American soil, and further. We are neighbors, and we uplift each other -- without that, what are we then?

name: The Cheering Crowd
location_name: 112 W Lexington Ave, Independence, MO 64050, USA
description: "The Cheering Crowd" captures the energy, diversity, and unity of Kansas City's sports fans. Stylized silhouettes of cheering fans and iconic athletes wrap around the heart, symbolizing the connection between community and the games we love.

name: KC Garden of Hearts
location_name: 5909 Raytown Trafficway, Raytown, MO 64133, USA
description: "KC Garden of Hearts" weaves together beloved symbols of Kansas City-through winding vines that represent connection and growth. The red hearts and heart-shaped center symbolize the city's strong, united spirit. This design honors the shared pride that brings our community together.

name: The Colors of KC.
location_name: 220 SW Main St, Lee's Summit, MO 64063, USA
description: Side 2 is reference to Kansas City's rich jazz tradition and the mighty MO it sits above. Side 1 is the beauty and wonder of the Country Club Plaza and Brush Creek that winds through it and its fountains and architecture. A heart with be in the opening that I hope will rotate. And on the base I tried to have the Mighty Mo/skyline go around its side to then blend into The Plaza side, which is the b

name: Sister Cities - We Are Family!
location_name: 927 NE Woods Chapel Rd, Lee's Summit, MO 64064, USA
description: Kansas City has 12 Sister Cities from across the globe celebrating all aspects of culture they have in common. From architecture to business; jazz music to fountains you will find we that we common interests with almost every culture in the world. Your love of sports may have brought you here but I hope you will explore our city and experience all the things we have in common. After all, we are fa

name: Vibrance
location_name: 1300 S Broadway, Oak Grove, MO 64075, USA
description: Vibrance celebrates Kansas City's First Fridays. using stylized negative space and color to represent a flourishing, diverse, and inclusive artistic community. Rich in culture, art, music, and food, our First Fridays have the kind of life and energy that can only be found here in America's heartland.

name: Rich Contributions
location_name: 650 Minnesota Ave #2, Kansas City, KS 66101, USA
description: I designed this heart to highlight the contributions of immigrants that make Kansas City and cities everywhere richer. The first side showcasing a traditional Mexican folk dancer and Kansas City silhouetted beside her represents the beautiful culture brought with them and shared amongst us. The second side depicts a Mexican farmer harvesting corn, one of the two main crops grown here, representing

name: A Colorful Mind, A Loving Heart
location_name: 548 Central Ave, Kansas City, KS 66101, USA
description: My heart design shows what it's like to have ADHD and a big imagination. The bright colors and patterns represent how my mind moves in lots of directions but is full of love. I want people to see that being different can be beautiful.

name: Soccer Unites
location_name: Barnett Ave W of N 18th St, Kansas City, KS
description: The two different sides represent the two soccer teams in Kansas City while the colors around the heart symbolize the colors of the old Wizards soccer team logo. The two sides have different angles of the city to show the two teams on different states sharing the same city. The soccer balls look different, One being old and one new to represent the past and the future of soccer in KC.

name: Goals In The Sky - A Kansas City Dreamscape
location_name: 4601 State Ave, Kansas City, KS 66102, USA
description: Inspired by the magic of Van Gogh's "Starry Night", "Goals in the Sky - A Kansas City Dreamscape" comes to life. Illuminated by starry nights and stadium lights, Van Gogh meets game day, in a high-energy tribute to Kansas City's electric heartbeat, and moment to shine as host city for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Kansas City steps into the global spotlight as a vibrant canvas where nations will gather

name: UNTETHERED
location_name: 1120 E Old, MO-210 Liberty, MO 64068
description: U N T E T H E R E D heart, a free heart that flows with love and freedom of expression. It's untamed, unlimited, unrestricted... every line unique and unpredictable, connected yet independent. Hidden are the words of the world... 17 within this painting, different languages, bridging the gaps of culture and language.

name: Hopes & Struggles
location_name: 249 W Mill St unit 109, Liberty, MO 64068, USA
description: "Hopes & Struggles" focuses on highlighting the historical contributions of Clay County African Americans acknowledging the rich history, celebrating the culture then and now and promoting human relationships while breaking down barriers.

name: Remembrance of the Heart
location_name: 1703 E 18th St, Kansas City, MO 64108, USA
description: Remembrance of the Heart of Kansas City pays homage to the vibrant history of the jazz district and the Crossroads Art District, once known as Film Row. This sculpture celebrates the area's rich cultural legacy, from the jazz and steamboat era to its role in Black cinema, uniting the past and present through art and community.

name: Oops I Dripped It Again
location_name: 208 W Lexington Ave, Independence, MO 64050, USA
description: The goal of Oops I Dripped It Again is to draw viewers in with the vibrant rainbow drips then reveal to them the hidden gems of Kansas City with the pattern underneath. It is a reminder of the fun, playful, and whimsical side of life and invites you to find joy in the small things, like a soulful song, a beautiful flower, or a good 'ol plate of Kansas City BBQ.

name: Fore the Love of KC
location_name: 1900 Diamond Pkwy, North Kansas City, MO 64116, USA
description: Fore the Love of KC celebrates the community's love for Kansas City, its passion for all sports, and the growing inclusivity that welcomes everyone to play. The main feature of the sculpture is a large golf ball, surrounded by bird's-eye views of a golf course, while the base features a flat lay of tees, a score card, cart key, and classic argyle pattern. The design blends nostalgic golf imagery w

name: Welcome to the Party!
location_name: 20 E 5th St, Kansas City, MO 64106, USA
description: A colorful celebration of KCMO's one of a kind vibrance as a community! The neon colors symbolize the electric energy of the city. The free-flowing, overlapping patterns are an interpretation of the blend of beautiful cultures that thrive in our communities. The disco ball embodies the excitement of the fun and possibilities on the night spent out in Kansas City!

name: In Flanders Fields
location_name: 4525 Oak St, Kansas City, MO 64111, USA
description: Inspired by the poem written in WWI, 'In Flanders Fields' represents strength and peace, similar to what Kansas City is for many people. With a blue sky and blooming poppies, the end is reminiscent of the place many of us call home.

name: The Heart of Bellefontaine
location_name: E 53rd St & Troost Ave, Kansas City, MO 64110, USA
description: The 1928 animated short Steamboat Willie, featuring the original depiction of Mickey Mouse, has officially entered the U.S. public domain. This heart celebrates that milestone by showcasing characters from the classic film alongside nods to Walt Disney's early years in Kansas City. It includes references to Bellefontaine Avenue (where he lived), Troost Avenue (home of his studio), and Kansas City'

name: Unleashing Kansas City
location_name: 4233 Roanoke Rd ste 200, Kansas City, MO 64111, USA
description: A vibrant celebration of Kansas City's spirit, wrapped in the leash of is loyal companions. Each dog portrait leads to a story- from barbeque bites to jazz nights, Royals blue to fountain hues- capturing Kansas City's soul through the collars, bandanas, and bows of its four-legged locals.

name: Color Burst
location_name: 9119 E 39th St, Kansas City, MO 64133, USA
description: When I was a kid, my favorite part about going downtown was looking at all the graffiti and street art displayed across the buildings of Kansas City. I was inspired by the bold colors and stylized characters that reflected how vibrant the art community of KC is. I remember wishing that I could create a work of art similar to these. This design takes inspiration from the murals I loved so much when

name: A Light in the Darkness
location_name: 1 Wild Horse Pkwy Greenwood, MO 64034
description: My work represents through words and images all the characteristics that should be reflected in our society. It exemplifies the idea of finding peace through the images of a dove and a peace sign surrounded in native flora of the midwest. It is a wish to make the world a better place using imagery with multiple meanings like the monarch butterfly, dandelion, or the lit candle raised to light the w

name: Sweethearts
location_name: 503 W Main St, Greenwood, MO 64034, USA
description: Sweethearts is a sculpture that seeks to inspire others to be more loving and to kind to others. The messages on the hearts are positive affirmations that speak positivity over our city. They wanted to create a piece that brings our city together.

name: Sunrise/Sunset
location_name: 8771 W 95th St, Overland Park, KS 66212, USA
description: The heart is a celebration of native regional wildlife in Kansas City's two states and their respective ecosystems. Facing west towards the sunset, the heart is a window into the Kansas plains and prairie; facing east, the sun rises over the green woodlands and forests of Missouri.

name: The Heart of Us
location_name: 10118 W 119th St, Overland Park, KS 66213, USA
description: My heart's design represents Kansas City itself and celebrates all who were not born here but chose it. For all the new travelers visiting Kansas City and for all the people that fell in love with its charms and decided to stay, you are welcome in our home and our hearts.

name: Beyond the Fairway
location_name: 13500 Nall Ave, Overland Park, KS 66224, USA
description: My sculpture design features an acrylic painting of a golfer on one side, captured in the moment just after hitting the perfect shot. At the base, a detailed putting green with a hole invites viewers to imagine the final challenge of the game, emphasizing precision and patience. On the opposite side, the sculpture is painted like a giant golf ball, its dimples textured and detailed, with a bold he

name: Kansas City Melody
location_name: 13800 Switzer Rd, Overland Park, KS 66221, USA
description: This heart is a celebration of Kansas City's rich jazz heritage, filled with flowing musical notes in vibrant orange and blue. It reflects the city's rhythm, energy, and soulful spirit, connecting music, color, and community in a playful, dynamic design.

name: Festival of Connecting and Celebrating Cultures
location_name: 8018 W 151st St, Overland Park, KS 66223, USA
description: What better way to show unity than by the city's melting pot of culture brought together every year in an immersive fun-filled weekend at cultural festivals in KC. On one side of the sculpture shows performances, activities, and food we would experience. The other side presents a parade of flags, much like the parade of hearts, which shows the love and pride for our differences and what we celebra

name: K C N Tees
location_name: 16655 W 151st St, Olathe, KS 66062, USA
description: Kansas City Hot Spots- in Tees. See if you can identify some of KC's best destinations. Much like Kansas City, there is a souvenir T-shirt for every interest and every person!

name: Soccer United
location_name: 1 Sporting Way, Kansas City, KS 66111, USA
description: Soccer United is celebrating the World Cup in 2026 and how the 3 countries are hosting them.

name: Third Eye Spin Cycle
location_name: 1100 N 98th St, Kansas City, KS 66111, USA
description: "Open Heart Frequency" is a vibrant, psychedelic tribute to love, music, and spiritual connection. Drawing from the visual language of folk art, mysticism, and vintage record culture, the piece radiates with color, symbolism, and movement. This piece reflects the city's rich creative energy and celebrates the universal heartbeat that connects us all, a kaleidoscopic beacon of unity in the heart of

name: Imam te u srcu/I have you in my heart
location_name: 1841 Village West Pkwy, Kansas City, KS 66111, USA
description: A heart sculpture representing the Croatians who settled Strawberry Hill. The front incorporates motifs of traditional Croatian embroidery and the back is a Croatian licitar cookie. The base represents Strawberry Hill itself.

name: Touching Color, Listening to Imagination
location_name: 15520 State Ave, Basehor, KS 66007, USA
description: This Heart features bold, abstract blocks of color with rich, varied textures, inviting touch as a way for views to experience its composition beyond sight. At it's center rests a tactile sphere with Braille offering a personal excerpt from a recorded conversation between BeiBei, Lisa and Katy, accessed via QR code, expands the piece into sound, encouraging audiences to explore art through multipl

name: Pigeon Flyover
location_name: 10541 S Warwick St, Olathe, KS 66061, USA
description: "Pigeon Flyover" features a sculpted pigeon flying triumphantly through the open center of the heart, framed by a magical Midwest sunset, and a bird's eye view of downtown and the West Bottoms rendered in brilliant color. More members of the pigeon flock make themselves at home on the sculpture's base.

name: Take Heart & Explore
location_name: 11180 S Lone Elm Rd, Olathe, KS 66061, USA
description: Exploration. Imagination. Adventure. Universal themes that resonate in the hearts and minds of all ages across time, spaces, and places. The goal of this bright, inviting heart, "Take Heart & Explore", is to represent the child-like enthusiasm that burns within all of us. We strive to imagine what new spaces, in the heartland and beyond, are out there to see... to explore the places that are beyon

name: Bits & Pieces
location_name: 233 S Cherry St, Olathe, KS 66061, USA
description: Bits & Pieces is a mosaic collage created from second hand glass and ceramic from Kansas City's residents. A sort of community project, Bits & Pieces reminds us of the harmony that can be created when we all band together, no matter how different we may be.

name: Behind The Redline
location_name: 12401 E US Hwy 40, Independence, MO 64055, USA
description: A Black man and woman face away beneath a sunset, with a golden key between them. A cutout reveals neighborhood homes, symbolizing Black labor without ownership. Bricks and bold patterns reflect struggle, strength, and cultural legacy.

name: Let Your Heart Lead The Way
location_name: 504 NE 70th St, Kansas City, MO 64118, USA
description: Colorful color block background with wraparound type on both sides with a retro vibe. Wraparound script type on base.

name: United in the Heartland
location_name: 3200 Ameristar Dr, Kansas City, MO 64161, USA
description: Kansas City is home to one of the most welcoming and diverse cities, so I wanted to design my heart around that. I want anyone who sees my heart to know that no matter who you are or where you're from, you're welcomed. Represent our roots in music, sports, and show friendliness.

name: Upcycled Flora Missouri State Flower, White Hawthorn
location_name: 209 SE Green St, Lee's Summit, MO 64063, USA
description: My design features the heart sculpture adorned with hand-painted flowers crafted entirely from recycled plastic bottles. Each bloom represents the native flower of Missouri, the White Hawthorn, celebrating the state's natural beauty while highlighting environmental responsibility. The repurposed bottles, beneath the soft white, yellow, pink and green paint, give the piece a textured, 3D surface th

name: Where Lives Meet
location_name: 425 NE Mock Ave, Blue Springs, MO 64014, USA
description: This design celebrates the vibrant tapestry of life in Kansas City, where every color represents a unique story. The overlapping shapes symbolize the way our lives intersect-some encounters brief and fleeting, others leaving a lasting mark that becomes part of the city's permanent fabric. Together, these layers of color capture the beauty, diversity, and connection that make Kansas City a place wh

name: Inclusion Revolution
artist: Andres Rivas-Cruz, Marc Harrell, Abby Martin, Ali O'Grady, JoAnna Long
location_name: Kansas City, KS 66101
description: Kansas City leads the Inclusion Revolution with a bold, open-hearted spirit. Here, people of all abilities aren't just welcomed, they're celebrated.

name: Heartland Patchwork
location_name: 1420 S 78 St, Kansas City, KS 66111, USA
description: One side of Heartland Patchwork welcomes visitors with alternating sawtooth stars and a frayed four patch chain quilt blocks in blues and golds while the other side is made of alternating red and yellow pinwheels. The top of the base represents a double wedding ring quilt and the sides of the base are wrapped in flying geese blocks. The repeating yellow borders provide consistency and cohesion to

name: Kansas City Sunset
location_name: 18001 Bass Pro Dr, Independence, MO 64055, USA
description: Kansas City skyline in bold silhouettes, set against a vibrant sunset with iconic landmarks.

name: Fred Harvey and the Harvey Girls - a Story of Entrepreneurship, the Railroad, and Romance.
location_name: 304 E 4th St, Tonganoxie, KS 66086, USA
description: Our design pays tribute to the rich history of Kansas City, Missouri, and its neighboring city of Leavenworth, Kansas. It tells the story of entrepreneur Fred Harvey, the founding father of the American service industry, and the intriguing Harvey Girls. It is a story of entrepreneurship, the railroad, and romance.

name: Inclusion Starts with the Heart
location_name: 901 Massachusetts St, Lawrence, KS 66044, USA
description: "Inclusion Starts with the Heart" is a tribute to individuals with disabilities, symbolizing compassion, connection, and the importance of seeing the person first.

name: Happy Creation
location_name: 724 Main St, Eudora, KS 66025, USA
description: "Happy Creation" represents a constant movement of transformation, in which everything is born, transformed, and renewed. It reminds us that true creation flows from the heart. The Heart (the origin of all life energy) Silver (moonlight) & Gold (the sun) intertwines harmoniously between the duality of the lunar and solar, shapes are non-figurative, rather like living energy, reminding us that crea

name: Reach for the Heart
location_name: 33180 W 83rd St, De Soto, KS 66018, USA
description: Reach for the Heart celebrates the therapeutic and empowering nature of climbing, where every move reflects persistence and self-discovery. Inspired by Kansas City's vibrant climbing community, it embodies the encouragement, trust, and connection shared between climbers. This heart is a tribute to resilience and the joy of reaching toward both personal goals and the hearts of others.

name: The World's Heartstrings
location_name: 126 E Cedar St, Bonner Springs, KS 66012, USA
description: The World's Heartstrings features a painted world map composed of hundreds of colorful, people-like figures, forming a mosaic of humanity that replaces borders with community. A swirling musical staff wraps around the heart, connecting continents and figures, with color-coded notes embedded throughout. These notes correspond to eight tone pipes suspended inside the heart's opening, which can be pl

name: Sending Love from the Heart
location_name: 631 N 126th St Bonner Springs, KS 66012
description: "Sending Love from the Heart" is a tribute to Kansas City's diversity and love. This design takes us back to the timeless art of postage stamps illustrating a common culture, history and spirit interwined by two states that split a city.

name: Wyandotte Gardens
location_name: 941 N 74 Dr, Kansas City, KS 66112, USA
description: Wyandotte Gardens is a dedication to my neighborhood and community. It is also a dedication to Deputy Elijah Ming who made the ultimate sacrifice protecting our community and helping a woman escape domestic violence. I am hoping my art is the spark to reignite the neighborhood group that hasn't been active in years and create a safe space for community connection and hope for our future.

name: Golden Heart of KC
location_name: 840 S 55th St, Kansas City, KS 66106, USA
description: My sculpture is painted in radiant gold and covered in shimmering rhinestones to catch the light from every angle. Suspended in the center by two gold chains are bold "KC" letters, representing Kansas City pride and unity. Set on a sleek golden base, the piece blends luxury with local love in a striking, modern statement.

name: Inspiration Is Everywhere
location_name: 2010 N 59 St, Kansas City, KS 66104, USA
description: My heart design is inspired by my elementary art teacher and my elementary music teacher. Both of these individuals helped my creative side bloom and made me realize my love for the art world. They both inspired me with their beautifully bold authentic personalities to be myself unapologetically.

name: Golden Gathering
location_name: 14730 K-68 Hwy, Louisburg, KS 66053, USA
description: Whether it's a wedding, a concert, or a comedy show, the Midland theater's ornate, shimmering details always catch my breath. In my own work, I found inspiration in the Midland's French Baroque style. The deep reds and glowing gold décor feel like they hold stories in their folds. I wanted to echo that warmth and dynamism, capturing not just the look, but the spirit of community that fills every c

name: Sister Cities
location_name: 199th & Metcalf Ave, Bucyrus, KS 66013
description: Side one: Painting of Sister Cities Bridge which crosses over Brush Creek on The Plaza. Side two: Painting of the twelve flags representing Kansas City's twelve sister cities. Base: Painting of a Channel Catfish and Trout in yin-yang fashion on the top with the names of our twelve sister cities wrapping the sides.

name: Kansas City Kindness
location_name: 506 Main St, Belton, MO 64012, USA
description: Give people flowers and kindness while you can. You never know when that opportunity can be taken away! Kindness is so important, and Kansas City does kindness the best.

name: Hearts & Crafts
location_name: 9502 Lake Lotawana Rd, Lake Lotawana, MO 64086, USA
description: This heart celebrates the joy of making and the unity found in crafting. Featuring vibrant patterns of sewing, crochet, embroidery, and other craft tools, each side blooms with handmade charm, the state flowers of Kansas and Missouri under the same blue sky. The design honors the hands that stitch, glue, cut, and create-reminding us that crafting connects hearts, one thread at a time.

name: Before They Call Your Name
location_name: 301 S Bynum Rd, Lone Jack, MO 64070, USA
description: My Parade of Hearts design blends play and cosmic imagery to explore what remains after presence and biology. Deep blue and purple galaxies dotted with gummy worms surround looping rods with colorful beads that invite touch and movement. Phrases across the heart remind viewers that love and memory can outlast time, distance, and even death.

name: Noah's Legacy
artist: Lindsay Frack, Prairie Center Elementary Students
location_name: 13518 S Alden St, Olathe, KS 66062, USA
description: Noah, age 6, was receiving treatment for cancer at Children's Mercy and noticed a need for child-like colorful bandages. He reached out to the Royals, his community and friends. From there, a legacy known as Noah's Bandage Project was born.

name: "Bee" Part of Our Hive
location_name: 750 The Paseo, Kansas City, MO 64106, USA
description: The bee hive is a symbol of community, while the bee represents the importance of each member within that community. Without you and I, there is no community.

name: Head in the Clouds
location_name: 329B E Gregory Blvd, Kansas City, MO 64114, USA
description: ""Head in the Clouds"" celebrates next-level love that is true, big, and life-changing-the kind that helps us feel truly connected and fulfilled. The cloud-painted ring represents how transformative love, whether romantic or communal, lifts us above life's challenges while keeping us grounded in unity. At the center, a stained glass heart with ""KC"" etched in the middle symbolizes the profound co

name: Heart of the Plaza
location_name: Emanuel Cleaver II Blvd. & Main St, Kansas City, MO 64112, USA
description: This heart is inspired by the Kansas City Plaza. This work uses imagery of the Mill Creek and Neptune fountains. Tiles are inspired by designs found on roofs, as well as symbols that correspond with the surrounding area.

name: WHERE IS THE JARIPEO???
location_name: 9100 Ward Pkwy, Kansas City, MO 64114, USA
description: This heart is an homage to the current and past western culture of Kansas City and its surrounding areas. This includes the recent boom in Jaripeos that gives Mexican-Americans in the area a little taste of Mexico. Its a beautiful reminder that cowboy culture is one that is shared between the US and Mexico.

name: The Heart of the Story
location_name: 6242 Swope Pkwy, Kansas City, MO 64130, USA
description: On the front, a silhouetted figure reads peacefully in Loose Park as the Kansas City skyline and KC Wheel rise behind them, evoking the excitement and wonder that books can inspire. The back side leans into fantasy, with a winding path, a mysterious door, and a white rabbit guiding the way-a nod to Kansas City's new children's literacy museum, The Rabbit Hole, and the magic of childhood imaginatio

name: The Wishing Coin
location_name: 3000 Troost Ave, Kansas City, MO 64109, USA
description: The Wishing Coin is an ancient stone-carved "coin" that blends Kansas City's history and culture into a single interactive sculpture. The heart-shaped centerpiece spins between the Ancient KC Wolf and Cowtown history, inviting visitors to "flip a coin" for fun or to guide their next big decision. Detailed ""carvings"" showcase KC's art deco style, jazz, sports, BBQ, and other beloved symbols, with

name: Intertwined
location_name: 10207 N Oak Trafficway, Kansas City, MO 64155, USA
description: In a flurry of color, 'Intertwined' loudly and proudly tells its message - love one another, be kind, and be proud! The hands surrounding the keyhole heart all show different symbols of love across cultures and languages, prompting the viewer to add their own. 'Intertwined's colors and free-flowing organic shapes are there to remind us to not take everything so seriously and spread a little more l

name: We Build This City
location_name: 800 E 101st Terrace, Kansas City, MO 64131, USA
description: The trades of Kansas City inspired me for this piece because they have built many of the buildings that I believe are at the heart of Kansas City. These tradespeople are a huge part of our community. They show up every day to create safe spaces for thousands of complete strangers. This piece exhibits all of the different roles that aid in the construction of our city.

name: Seeds of Resistance
location_name: 3013 Main St, Kansas City, MO 64108, USA
description: Stained glass mosaic of a watermelon over heart form

name: The Beautiful Game
location_name: 4057 Pennsylvania Ave, Kansas City, MO 64111, USA
description: My heart shows the Pride flag with the KC Current logo, along with the words "One World-One Sport-One Love", celebrating diversity in soccer in the KC, the U.S. and the world. My heart shows the love for "the beautiful game" as soccer/futbol is known for. The KC Current are making history and advances in women's soccer and for ALL women regardless of how they identify.

name: Can't Spell Lucky Without KC
location_name: 5930 Troost Ave, Kansas City, MO 64110, USA
description: Inspired by the rustic, hand-painted signs that are sprinkled around town, this heart captures the magic of Kansas City. The subtle rainbow palette and cross-stitch style lettering acknowledge the diverse roots of our community. How lucky are we to live in a place full of vibrant people, places, and things?

name: Valkommen to KC!
location_name: 1200 Oread Ave, Lawrence, KS 66044, USA
description: The Dala horse, or in this case, a Dala heart, is a Swedish symbol of good luck, strength, and warm wishes. Often found on the front of houses, they bear the family name or a warm Valkommen (welcome).

name: Handmade
location_name: 5261 N Antioch Rd, Kansas City, MO 64119, USA
description: The hands of this piece showcase Kansas City as a collective, which is comprised of a wide variety of individuals banded together as one. We are the beating part of KC that makes it whole - delicately holding our heart out to those around us. This culture of kindness is what makes the city uniquely colorful and strangely intertwined.

name: Heart of the City.
location_name: 1100 Main St, Kansas City, MO 64105, USA
description: This design was conceived as a way to properly represent the people and locals of Kansas City. The heart in the center is there to represent the people who make the city what it is, with the sky being a hopeful and optimistic blue, and the city itself being the border, holding everything inside. We're in this together!

name: I Still Have A Dream
location_name: 916 E 5th St, Kansas City, MO 64106, USA
description: Multiple images of Martin Luther King Junior depicted on the front, and his most famous speech "I Have A Dream" handwritten on the back. This heart not only represents the path that MLK Jr. paved, but also how his words inspired and continue to inspire the generations that followed him.

name: Ad Astra Per Aspera
location_name: 7935 Santa Fe Dr, Overland Park, KS 66204, USA
description: My heart was created to embody the state motto, "Ad Astra Per Aspera" ("to the stars through difficulties"), specifically the way our global community must work together to continue to transcend the challenges of humanity. Cosmic imagery is joined by natural landscape on one side, and iconic buildings from the downtown skyline on the other, to represent both the unity and diversity of our metro ar

name: CommUnity Through CommonUnity
location_name: 4801 Rockhill Rd, Kansas City, MO 64110, USA
description: Kansas City was built on division, but today a movement toward unity is taking root - from the Parade of Hearts to the Rock Island Bridge to Sporting KC's call of "Two States, One City." By finding common ground and embracing our differences, we turn them into sources of strength. United, we have the power to shape a stronger city, nation, and world.

name: Lending a Heart
location_name: 1701 Jarboe St, Kansas City, MO 64108, USA
description: This piece shows the hands of healthcare workers and their patients, forming a heart in a show of unity and compassion. The variation of gloved hands show all types of doctors, nurses, techs, and specialists. The patients hands show that we all come from different phases of life and different communities, but we all need care, support, and need our healthcare workers.

name: The JOY of Reading
location_name: 2816 W 47th Ave, Kansas City, KS 66103, USA
description: The JOY of Reading heart is a tribute to reading and the importance of coming together to share, connect and foster engagement. Our weekly series "What We're Reading Wednesday" will go through each of the 52 books displayed on the heart. I encourage the community to come together and experience the JOY of reading in their own special way while connecting to others through storytelling.

name: The Grass is Greener in KC
location_name: 315 N Pennsylvania Ave, Lawson, MO 64062, USA
description: My heart shows a community member watering their garden; immersed in the growth and life that surrounding them.

name: Heartland Toile
location_name: 6937 Tomahawk Rd, Prairie Village, KS 66208, USA
description: Inspired by vintage toile and chinoiserie patterns, this heart features beloved Kansas City landmarks in a dreamy blue-and-white palette. I love toile. It's beautiful, classic, and full of storytelling. I also wanted to share its history with my students, teaching them about its roots in 18th-century France and the tradition of pattern as a form of visual narrative. Each vignette was hand-drawn an

name: To Kansas City and Beyond
location_name: 4523 NW Gateway Ave, Riverside, MO 64150, USA
description: This space explorer and out-of-this-world design captures the unstoppable momentum of Kansas City-a city that continues to grow, evolve, and reach new heights. From Super Bowl victories and international recognition to modern airports, dynamic entertainment hubs, streetcar expansions, and the honor of hosting the FIFA World Cup, Kansas City is proving time and again: there's nothing we can't do. T

name: From Kansas City, With Love
location_name: 22220 Midland Dr, Shawnee, KS 66226, USA
description: From BBQ and Jazz to the Kansas City Chiefs and Union Station, Kansas City is home to many iconic symbols, landmarks, and traditions. Through the use of 16 unique and colorful stamps, my goal with this design is to show the world what makes Kansas City great.

name: The Beauty of Flavor
location_name: 11110 Johnson Dr, Shawnee, KS 66203, USA
description: BBQ is a KC love language. We share culture with all our friends, family, and neighbors in the form of a hearty meal.

name: Lighting The Kansas City Sky
location_name: 18419 Collins Rd, Smithville, MO 64089, USA
description: My heart pulls inspiration from the iconic Western Auto sign that illuminates the downtown skyline. One side of the heart captures the boiling and surrounding downtown landscape in a painterly manner while allowing the sign to be full visible a top the heart cutout. The other side takes the swoosh of the sign and highlights and surrounds the heart cutout, making it apart of the signing, making it

name: KC Lake Life
location_name: 118 N Commercial Ave, Smithville, MO 64089, USA
description: "Lake Life" is all about those epic sunsets over the lake, with the sky turning all kinds of orange and pink while cattails sway in the breeze. It's the perfect vibe when you're reeling in a bass or just hanging out with family, soaking up the peaceful scene. The warm sunset colors and tall cattails make you think of those awesome times at the lake. The goal is that when people see the heart, they

name: Lucky Penny
location_name: 507 Edmond St, St Joseph, MO 64501, USA
description: The design I have submitted is a "lucky penny" with a heart cut out of the center. It includes different tones of copper, as well as varying shades of brown to give it that worn/used look. I included 2026 as the year, with the letters "KC" underneath, and the phrase, "Find a penny pick it up... all day long you'll have good luck" on the base.

name: Heartland in Bloom
location_name: 401 Main St, Weston, MO 64098, USA
description: In a world often clouded by gloom, Heartland in Bloom invites viewers to push back against the clouds and embrace the vibrant landscape of Kansas City. Featuring two playful characters (each a nod to our local sports teams) the sculpture encourages people to step in, feel at home, and become part of the KC family.

name: NUESTRO ORIGEN
location_name: Red Barn Farm, 16300 Wilkerson Rd, Weston, MO 64098, USA
description: My heart design merges the Olmec heads with soccer, reflecting the origin and journey of our culture. Titled Nuestro Origen, it symbolizes where we come from and where we are going. The piece combines traditional Mexican patterns with ancient hieroglyphic-inspired elements for a bold, cultural fusion.

name: The Water Lilies
location_name: 8671 NW Prairie View Rd, Kansas City, MO 64153, USA
description: This heart features scenery from the Monet Gardens at the Overland Park Arboretum combined with the style of Claude Monet's Water lilies. Kansas City's own Nelson-Atkins is home to one of Monet's original water lily pieces that have amazed and inspired those who view it. By combining elements of Kansas City's natural beauty and famous paintings, the work highlights our artistic and creative cultur

name: Growth in Stages
location_name: 707 Webster St, Chillicothe, MO 64601, USA
description: This pieces celebrates the life cycle of key crop core to Kansas. Using brilliant warm hues of gold and yellow, the stages of growth are fully represented, showcased on a sea of blues.

name: The World's Rhythm
location_name: 201 E Broadway Ave, Excelsior Springs, MO 64024, USA
description: My design captures the vibrant and energetic spirit of FIFA, celebrating the diverse backgrounds of players and fans alike. Through dynamic patterns and bold colors, the design welcomes people from all corners of the globe to Kansas City. It's a visual celebration of unity and the shared joy of the beautiful game.

name: Read It Forward
location_name: 2706 W 53rd St, Fairway, KS 66205, USA
description: "Read It Forward" is a celebration of community, curiosity, and the magic of shared stories. Woven with Kansas City details, the sculpture serves as a Little Free Library and invites neighbors into a space of creativity and connection. Take a book, leave a book, and keep the stories moving.

name: Kansas City Is For Lovers
location_name: 1703 E 18th St, Kansas City, MO 64108, USA
description: "Kansas City Is For Lovers" is a celebration of the romantic inspiration found throughout our city, and is designed to be an interactive frame for photos. The heart is influenced by the regal look of blue and white porcelain delftware, combined with sketchy-stylized line work covering the whole heart with fun, abstract detail! Look closely to find hidden gems and symbols, and strike a pose inside

name: Beer Me, KC
location_name: 2010 Vine St building 2a, Kansas City, MO 64108, USA
description: "Beer Me, KC" is a bold, light hearted tribute to the city that knows how to raise a glass. This piece embodies the spirit of Kansas City; warm, welcoming, and always down for a good time.

name: International Heartland
location_name: 1709 Walnut St, Kansas City, MO 64108, USA
description: "International Heartland" highlights both Kansas City's growing global presence as a World Cup host city and its relevance in the American Midwest. The front of my design centers on the Kansas City skyline, an iconic meeting point for citizens of both Kansas and Missouri. A soccer ball, with a trail of colors reminiscent of the 2026 World Cup branding, swoops around to embrace the city, uniting ou

name: Grow Your Giving. Grow Your Heart. Grow Our City
location_name: 1055 Broadway Blvd #130, Kansas City, MO 64105, USA
description: This heart sculpture celebrates generosity, community, and growth-values at the core of the Greater Kansas City Community Foundation's mission to help individuals and families Grow Their Giving. Rooted in symbolism, the base of the heart is covered in real pennies, representing the foundational power of every gift-big or small-and honoring the countless acts of generosity that have shaped our regi

name: Peace, Love and Kansas City
location_name: 410 NW Legacy Dr, Kansas City, MO 64155, USA
description: A peace sign with Kansas City themed doodle art on a background of the progress pride flag colors.

name: The World We Play In
location_name: 8081 NW Roanridge Rd., Kansas City, MO 64151, USA
description: My heart design is a showcase of some of the magnificent structures throughout the world. I also want to show how the sea keeps us all connected. With my love of sports and the World Cup coming to Kansas City, I made the base a soccer pitch...I love the idea that we can set our world differences aside for a couple weeks and all come together to ""play a game"".

name: An Enduring Connection
location_name: 400 S Jefferson St, Kearney, MO 64060, USA
description: This heart blends the nostalgia of friendship bracelets with the iconography of KC to capture the spirit of the heartland that welcomes all.

name: The Gold Within
location_name: 8710 Lackman Rd, Lenexa, KS 66219, USA
description: The Gold Within" is a golden heart sculpture with a true mirror embedded in its center, inviting viewers to see themselves as they truly are. Inspired by my viral art series where I paint strangers in gold while hearing their stories, this piece turns that experience into a public, communal reflection. It's a tribute to the idea that our greatest value has always lived within us - sometimes we jus

name: The Nelson: Confluence of Dreams
location_name: NW Corner of Merriam Dr & Johnson Dr. Merriam, KS 66202
description: The Nelson-Atkinson Museum of Art - north and south entrances with reflecting pool, lawn and shuttlecocks

name: KC Dreaming
location_name: 8805 Shawnee Mission Pkwy, Merriam, KS 66202, USA
description: The KC Dreaming design is inspired by merging the Kansas City of today with a whimsical, futuristic vision of happiness and harmony - and a bit of silliness with dwellers of the metro in space suits. KC Dreaming depicts snapshots of Kansas City, children at play, and space-suited Kansas Citians enjoying and exploring the city.

name: Capture the Moment
location_name: 5612 Johnson Dr, Mission, KS 66202, USA
description: My heart is intended to capture the moment in KC the city of fountains. The side of the the hearts has the marking of a camera lens, with the front of the heart representing the shutter of a camera. The back of the heart makes the shape of fountains flowing in the shape of a heart.

name: Growing Light
location_name: 6000 College Blvd, Overland Park, KS 66211, USA
description: Native wildflowers bloom under a radiant Kansas City sunset, symbolizing growth, resilience, and the beauty rooted in community.

name: Home is Where My Heart Lies
location_name: 328 Main St, Platte City, MO 64079, USA
description: My heart features the names of regions residents in the KC area call home, iconography belonging to the two Midwest states the city stitches together, along with the beloved names given to the region as our Nation's Heartland City.

name: Purple Heart, Purple Soul
location_name: 2401 Kentucky Ave, Platte City, MO 64079, USA
description: The Purple Heart side represents an overall Love for Kansas City we all share. The Purple Soul side pays homage to iconic history of music Kansas city has.

name: Let's Play!
location_name: 100 Wyoming St, Pleasant Hill, MO 64080, USA
description: "Let's Play!" is an artwork created to bring cheer and uplift the viewer, leaving them with a sense of joy.

name: Driven By Love
location_name: 8040 US-69, Pleasant Valley, MO 64068, USA
description: This sculpture is finished in candy inspired multi-colored metallics featuring a flat silhouette resembling the iconic impala. Inspired by Kansas City's car scene, this piece honors the community where culture and creativity ride side by side.

name: Hey World, Welcome to Kansas City!
location_name: 4200 W 83rd St, Prairie Village, KS 66208, USA
description: My concept for Parade of Hearts Kansas City is a KCityscape bursting with pride as we host some of the many (78) World Cup 26 matches. The art is a pinwheel design highlighting the historic buildings, landmarks and art located throughout our town as we honor our world class visitors who are soaring to new heights in hopes of winning the coveted FIFA World Cup trophy.

name: Intuition
description: This piece is a visual meditation on the power of intuition.

name: Rhythm & Harmony
location_name: 20709 Harper Rd Peculiar, MO 64078
description: My heart is a reflection of the musical roots of our city, the richness of our culture and how music brings us together.

name: Crown Jewel of the Heartland
location_name: 1881 Main St Kansas City MO 64108
description: My heart design, the Crown Jewel of the Heartland, reflects the brilliance and resilience that make Kansas City truly shine. Like a finely cut diamond, each facet of this city-from its rich culture and soulful jazz to its bold spirit and deep-rooted pride-sparkles with authenticity and charm. Just as every gem tells a story, KC's energy, grit, and grace continue to shape mine in the most dazzling

name: Monarchs and Movement
location_name: 1616 E 18th St Kansas City MO 64108
description: This design is in honor of Kansas City's place in history as a place important to migration, represented by the stylized Monarch butterfly on one side and the other honoring the Kansas City Monarchs and their legacy within sports history.

name: Character Speaks
location_name: 1447 Liberty St, Kansas City, MO 64102, USA
description: My heart is inspired by the beautifully constructed older buildings and structures that stand within Kansas City. Whether you're downtown, strolling loose park or at the historic west bottoms, these beautifully mysterious older buildings that surrounds the area with unique detailing and history will never fail to catch your attention. These structures make up Kansas City and gift it so much charac

name: Cultivating Hope & Growing Harmony
location_name: 2506 Holmes St Kansas City MO 64108
description: This design symbolizes the nurturing of hope - like planting seeds of optimism and care - that blossoms into harmony, unity and diversity. Just as flowers, and winged creatures come together in a vibrant, colorful way, so to can people from all walks of life grow together in peace and understanding. Through hope, we cultivate a world where harmony thrives.

name: Change Your Perspective
location_name: W 39th Ave, Kansas City, MO 64111, USA
description: "Change Your Perspective" encourages viewers to look at things differently. When viewed straight on the hearts messages are an incoherent jumble, but if you step to the side and view it at a 45 degree angle the diagonal panels align and messages become readable. From two angles the heart appears closed off, reminding views to keep their heart open and choose empathy over hate.

name: Paris of the Plains
location_name: 928 Wyandotte St Kansas City MO 64105
description: The confluence of the Missouri and Kansas rivers plays sweet melodies across the plains and inspires the Jazz and cultural growth of KC in a regional style of Thomas Hart Benton

name: Fine Dining
location_name: 1123 Walnut St, Kansas City, MO 64106, USA
description: A good meal can bring all sorts of people together. Modeled after a formal porcelain plate, this piece is a labor of love to that very special Midwest hospitality and the universal love language of food. It's always fine dining if you're dining with fine company-pull up a chair, there's always room at the table.
